title: Jor Torlin
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jor}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{Character |type=Sith Empire |image=[[File:Jortorlin.jpg]] |name=Jor Torlin |homeworld=[[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]{{CSWECite|III|255|Torlin, Jor}} |birth= |death=[[137 ABY]], [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]][[Legacy (2006) 10|''Legacy'' (2006) 10]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]][[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[One Sith]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[Sith Intelligence and Assassination]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You ''do'' talk. You can even ''reason''. Perhaps you can ''be'' reasonable."''
"''I'll hear you out. I can always kill you ''later''.|Morrigan Corde and Jor Torlin|}} '''Jor Torlin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] who worked for the [[Sith Intelligence and Assassination]] division of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]]. He was raised on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and was initially [[Sith training|trained]] as a Sith until it was found that he lacked adequate [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]]. Instead, Torlin became a Sith [[Spy/Legends|spy]] and gained a reputation of being one of the foremost agents in the [[One Sith]]'s intelligence division, led by [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] [[Darth Maladi]]. In [[137 ABY]], Torlin was tasked with locating the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]]. After meeting with the [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] [[Information broker/Legends|informant]] [[Haako]] aboard the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]], Torlin encountered the retired [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agent [[Morrigan Corde]], who had been reactivated by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] Nyna Calixte to assist in locating Skywalker. The two agents discovered that negotiations for a potential alliance were taking place between [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]] and Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mingo Bovark]], who was loyal to deposed [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile]]. Torlin and Corde successfully managed to [[Skirmish on The Wheel|sabotage]] the meeting, causing the two factions to remain divided. After this, the two agents traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] to search for Skywalker. Torlin, who distrusted Corde, [[Mission to Ossus (137 ABY)|attempted]] to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the agent in Ossus's jungles. However, Corde activated a micro-ampule that was filled with a [[Poison/Legends|nerve toxin]] that she had inserted while he was [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]] aboard his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Raider (Gladius-class)|''Raider'']], and left Torlin to die as she commandeered the ''Raider'' and departed Ossus. ==Biography== ===Early life and career=== {{Quote|I've read your dossier. Force sensitive. Trained on Korriban by the Sith, though not strong enough in the Force to truly be Sith. Sith at heart, though, and good at what you do—and I know how the Sith value retribution and revenge.|Morrigan Corde|}} [[File:JorTorlin-LECG.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Jor Torlin]] Jor Torlin, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in the [[Time/Legends|decades]] leading up to the [[Sith–Imperial War]] between the [[Fel Empire]], led by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]], and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. In his [[Youngling/Legends|youth]], Torlin was raised on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and headquarters of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] Order. He was [[Sith training|trained]] in their ways, but it was discovered that he lacked sufficient [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] to truly become a Sith. He accepted a role as a Sith [[Agent/Legends|operative]], using his limited knowledge of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to assist him in his missions. Torlin carried out missions in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] on the Sith's behalf before they revealed themselves by allying with [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Fel's Empire [[128 BBY|one year]] into the Sith–Imperial War.[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] After the [[Battle of Caamas|defeat]] of the Galactic Alliance and Darth Krayt's [[Attack on Emperor Fel|overthrow]] of Roan Fel in [[130 ABY]], Torlin continued to operate on the new [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire's]] behalf, acting as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and locating people at various locations such as [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] and [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located in the [[Besh Gorgon system/Legends|Besh Gorgon system]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. When he found his targets, Torlin typically reported his intel to his superiors, most often to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] [[Darth Maladi]], who was the head of [[Sith Intelligence and Assassination]]. During his career, Torlin occasionally used the surveillance services of the [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] [[Information broker/Legends|informant]] [[Haako]], who lived aboard the Wheel. At some point during his career, Torlin acquired the [[Raider (Gladius-class)|''Raider'']], a [[Gladius-class light freighter|''Gladius''-class light freighter]], using the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] on his missions for the Sith. By [[137 ABY]], during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], when Maladi replaced most of the agents of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] with her own, Torlin was regarded as Maladi's preeminent operative. ===Search for Skywalker and death=== {{Quote|Story of your life, Torlin. Good, but not good enough.|Morrigan Corde|}} In 137 ABY, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Maladi assigned Torlin to search for the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]] on behalf of Darth Krayt, who sought to use Skywalker's [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Force healing/Legends|heal]] himself[[Legacy (2006) 7|''Legacy'' (2006) 7]] of the [[Yorik-Kul]] seeds that had been implanted in his body by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] over a century before, which threatened to overtake the Dark Lord's body and mind.[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]] Torlin was contacted by Haako, who informed the agent that Skywalker was aboard the Wheel. Torlin instructed Haako to follow Skywalker aboard the station, and by the time Torlin met with the Chadra-Fan to confirm that he indeed saw Skywalker, the former Jedi had left the station. After telling Haako to discover Skywalker's location and his companions' identities, Torlin was confronted by a group of [[Unidentified ruffian group|ruffians]]. He attacked [[Unidentified thug leader|their leader]] and proceeded to fight the group, before he was hit over the head with a metal object. At that moment, the formerly retired Imperial Intelligence spy [[Morrigan Corde]], who had been reactivated by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] Nyna Calixte to help locate Skywalker, appeared and aided Torlin in combating the thugs. After they fled from the two Imperial agents, Torlin attacked Corde, who had set up the confrontation between Torlin and the thugs in order to make contact with Torlin. During the struggle, Corde used the [[Stun gas|knockout gas]] contained in her left [[Limb/Legends|arm]] gauntlet to disable Torlin and carried the Sith spy back to his ship. After the security forces aboard the Wheel let Corde aboard Torlin's ship, Corde inserted a micro-ampule containing a [[Poison/Legends|nerve toxin]] into Torlin's body, which she could activate in case Torlin attempted to kill her again. [[File:Evilplans.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torlin and Morrigan Corde hack Fel-Imperial Captain Mingo Bovark's shuttle with the former's C.O.L.D. devices.]] Torlin awoke aboard the vessel strapped to a seat by Corde, who introduced herself and threatened to kill him. After he called her bluff, she released him, and the two began discussing Skywalker's possible location. Corde brought the presence of [[Unidentified Crix-class diplomatic courier shuttle|two]] [[Unidentified Nune-class Imperial shuttle|ships]] belonging to [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]] and Fel-Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mingo Bovark]], respectively, to Torlin's attention. Torlin and Corde came to the conclusion that the two leaders were discussing a potential alliance between the Galactic Alliance Remnant and Roan Fel's [[Empire-in-exile]], and they worked together to create a plan to break up the negotiations by playing off the two sides' mutual suspicions of the other. Torlin launched all six of his [[Command Override Limpet Droid]]s—devices which magnetically attached to a ship and enabled him to take control of the on-board [[Computer/Legends|computers]]—which all successfully attached to Bovark's [[Nune-class Imperial shuttle|shuttle]]. Torlin then took control of one of the ship's [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], and fired on the [[Crix-class diplomatic courier shuttle|Galactic Alliance starship]]. In the [[Skirmish on The Wheel|ensuing chaos]], Admiral Stazi attacked Captain Bovark, who had to be rescued by [[Antares Draco|the]] [[Ganner Krieg|two]] [[Imperial Knight]]s accompanying him. After successfully sabotaging the negotiations, Torlin caused the C.O.L.D. devices to detach from Bovark's [[Shuttle/Legends|ship]] and left the Wheel with Corde aboard the ''Raider''. When they arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], which held the ruins of the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]], Torlin scanned for heat signatures and found one in the jungles near the ruins. Despite Corde's suggestion that they look for Skywalker on the neutral planet of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], Torlin landed the ''Raider'' a few [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] away from the ruins. After Torlin and Corde disembarked the ''Raider'', the former [[Mission to Ossus (137 ABY)|turned against]] the latter and opened fire at her with his heavy [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. Torlin pursued as she retreated, eventually locating her near a boulder. As he took aim to kill her, he began feeling the effects of the poison from the micro-ampule, which she had released when he began firing at her. As he lay dying, Corde explained what was happening to him and took out a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] which displayed an image from her past as the [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kol Skywalker]] and [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of Torlin's target, Cade Skywalker. She then abandoned Torlin to his [[Death/Legends|death]], taking the ''Raider'' and departing Ossus. ==Personality and traits== [[File:Heisdead.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Torlin succumbs to the nerve toxin Corde activated when he attempted to kill her.]] {{Quote|You Sith son of a murglak! We're on the same ''side''!"''
"''Maybe we are. Maybe we aren't. Better not to risk it. Better you're dead.|Corde and Torlin, as the latter attempts to kill the former|}} Jor Torlin was a light-skinned Human male who had wavy [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Despite lacking enough sensitivity with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to be a true Sith, Torlin was dedicated to the Sith cause and retained their values as an agent of Sith Intelligence. His skills as an agent led to him being reckoned as the foremost of Darth Maladi's operatives in 137 ABY. Despite his dedication, he still accepted pay from the Sith for his services. He occasionally enlisted the services of the Chadra-Fan informant Haako, whose information and scouting services he used during his career as a Sith operative. Torlin was able to handle himself in difficult combat situations, and he objected after Morrigan Corde aided in dispatching a gang of miscreants aboard the Wheel. He mistrusted Corde, which led him to attack her twice, and he did not care whether she was his ally or not, feeling her death was better than taking the chances of her being a foe. Among Torlin's possessions were the ''Gladius''-class [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''Raider'', a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and multiple Command Override Limpet [[Droid/Legends|Droids]]. He spoke and understood [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], and used a heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] rifle during combat. ==Behind the scenes== Jor Torlin was created by [[John Ostrander]], the writer of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], for the series' [[Star Wars: Legacy: Trust Issues|''Trust Issues'']] story arc. He was drawn by [[Colin Wilson (artist)|Colin Wilson]] in the arc's [[Legacy (2006) 9|two]] [[Legacy (2006) 10|issues]]. Torlin later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. His surname was spelled as both "Tolin" and "Torlin" in ''Trust Issues, Part 1''; however, ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' confirmed that the latter was the correct spelling. ==Appearances== *[[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 10|''Legacy'' (2006) 10]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jor Torlin}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jor Torlin |es=Jor Torlin |pl=Jor Torlin }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Torlin, Jor}} [[Category:Assassins]] [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Intelligence personnel of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Scouts]] [[Category:Sith acolytes and apprentices]] [[Category:Sith of the One Sith]] [[Category:Spies]]
